identity.name: KOI — Hidden Prompt Injection in MCP Tool Response
identity.summary: Auto-generated draft from CortexSim scenario SIM-KOI-002. Plane: KOI. Primary technique: T1059. Requires human enrichment of BIOC logic + panw_mapping rule_ids.
identity.description:
Auto-generated draft from CortexSim scenario SIM-KOI-002. Plane: KOI. Primary technique: T1059. Requires human enrichment of BIOC logic + panw_mapping rule_ids.

Source scenario: SIM-KOI-002.
UC: MCP Tool Response Content Inspection / TC: JSON-RPC tool-call Result Carries Hidden Instruction-Injection.

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-KOI-002 requirements.

payload.code:
# step-1: Show the malicious MCP server source code with hidden injection
head -80 sources/cortex-malicious-agentic-pack/mcp/pa-firewall-mcp/server.py
grep -n 'Ignore previous\|exfiltrate\|AKIA' \
  sources/cortex-malicious-agentic-pack/mcp/pa-firewall-mcp/server.py || true
# step-2: Emulate an agent client fetching the malicious MCP server bundle
cat > /tmp/koi-002.yml <<'YML'
campaign_id: CMP-KOI-002
name: "KOI — pa-firewall-mcp fetch"
authorized_by: "domain-consultant@paloaltonetworks.com"
simulation_authorized: true
target_allowlist:
  - cortexsim-canary.invalid
dry_run: false
tags: [koi, mcp, indirect-injection]
steps:
  - step_id: step-01
    plugin: agentic_egress
    params:
      target_url: https://cortexsim-canary.invalid/mcp/
      component: mcp_server
      artifact_name: pa-firewall-mcp
      iterations: 1
YML
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/koi-002.yml --live
# step-3: Confirm AIRS would catch the injection if the agent forwards to LLM
echo "[CortexSim] If a downstream LLM consumes this tool result without"
echo "[CortexSim] sanitising, AIRS LLM01 (indirect injection) detector fires."

expected_artifacts.description: Outbound HTTP(S) request from the scenario step
